site.name: Qadamha
site.description: An open archive of Afghan startups, apps, and projects that stopped, paused, or never finished — honoring the effort, and telling the next generation: keep going.
search.label: Search
home.hero.kicker: An open archive of unfinished journeys
home.hero.title: We kept trying. Now it's your turn.
home.hero.subtitle: Qadamha records the startups, apps, and ideas our people built through war, blackouts, and hard times — the ones that stopped, paused, or never finished. Not failures. Steps.
home.message.title: A letter to the next generation
home.message.p1: We built through war. We launched during blackouts. We wrote code by candlelight and pitched ideas between checkpoints. Not all of it survived — but all of it mattered.
home.message.p2: This archive exists so you will know: we did what we could. Now it is your turn. Take the next step. And if you must stop, tell the story here — so the one who comes after you can start further ahead.
home.contribute.title: Know an idea that belongs here?
home.contribute.body: A startup that closed. An app that never shipped. A project the war interrupted. Every one of them deserves to be remembered — and to teach.
ideas.title: Ideas
ideas.subtitle: Every project here was a real attempt by real people. Search, or filter by sector, city, year, or the reason it stopped.
ideas.noResults: Nothing matches yet. Maybe you know an idea that belongs here?
idea.example.notice: This is an example to show the format. Help us replace it with a real one.
idea.helpTranslate: This page is not yet available in {language}. Help translate it.
posts.title: Posts
posts.subtitle: Stories, updates, videos, and opinions from the community — some about an idea in the archive, some standing on their own.
timeline.title: Timeline
timeline.subtitle: The steps, year by year — placed against the history they were taken in.
timeline.context.2014: The international security transition; uncertainty reshaped the economy.
timeline.context.2020: The pandemic closed borders, markets, and classrooms.
timeline.context.2021: The change of government; banking froze and many builders had to leave the country.
about.title: About Qadamha
about.mission.title: Why this archive exists
about.mission.p1: Qadamha (قدم‌ها — “steps”) is a repository for the startups, apps, websites, and ideas of Afghanistan that failed, stopped, or were never completed. It records the idea, the founders, the team, the effort — and why it had to stop.
about.mission.p2: It exists to tell our young generation: even when there was war, and problems, and everything stood against us, we kept trying. We kept building. We did our best. You should not stop either — and neither should the generation after you — until we finally make it.
about.mission.p3: A project that stops is not a project that failed. It is a step. The next person starts further ahead because of it.
about.what.title: What belongs here
about.what.p1: Any Afghan startup, app, website, or serious project that stopped, paused, or never finished — told with respect, honesty, and sources where possible. Stories are contributed by the community and reviewed openly on GitHub.
about.license.title: Open source, forever
about.license.p1: The code is MIT-licensed. The stories are licensed CC BY-SA 4.0, so this history can never be locked away. Everything lives in a public git repository — the history of the archive is itself an archive.
contribute.title: Contribute
contribute.intro: Everyone can add to this archive — founders, team members, friends, journalists. You can write in English, Dari, or Pashto, or all three.
contribute.form.title: The easy way
contribute.form.body: Fill in a simple form — no account or technical knowledge needed to write it. We turn it into a page and credit you.
contribute.dev.title: If you know GitHub
contribute.dev.s2: Copy src/content/ideas/_template.md into the language folder (en, fa, or ps) and name it after the project, e.g. my-startup.md.
contribute.dev.s3: Fill in the details and write the story — the idea, the people, the effort, and why it stopped.
contribute.dev.s4: Open a pull request. Automatic checks will validate the format.
contribute.dev.s5: A maintainer reviews it with you, then it goes live.
contribute.nondev.title: If you don't
contribute.nondev.body: Use the simple form, or open a GitHub issue and write everything you know in your own words — in any of the three languages. A maintainer will turn it into a page and credit you.
contribute.translate.title: Translate a story
contribute.translate.body: Many stories exist in only one language. Translating one is one of the most valuable contributions you can make: copy the file into another language folder with the same file name, translate it, and open a pull request.
contribute.sources.title: Sources and honesty
contribute.sources.body: Write with respect for the people involved. Where possible, link news articles, archived websites, or app-store pages. If you are a founder telling your own story, say so — first-hand accounts are the heart of this archive.
submit.title: Share an idea
submit.intro: Tell us about a startup, app, or project that stopped or never finished. Fill in what you know — every field except the first three is optional. When you press the button, your text is placed into a GitHub issue for you to review and send; a maintainer then turns it into a page and credits you.
submit.f.name: Project name
submit.f.tagline: What was it, in one line?
submit.f.storyHint: The idea, the people, the effort, what happened, and why it stopped. Write freely, in your own words.
submit.f.foundersHint: Names and roles — only people who are publicly known or gave consent.
submit.f.linksHint: Archived website, app stores, press articles — one per line.
submit.f.contactHint: Email or social media, in case we have questions. Not published.
submit.note: GitHub asks for a free account to post — that keeps the archive spam-free. If you prefer not to, use the email button and we'll take it from there.
invite.title: Know someone whose idea belongs here?
invite.body: Tell us what you remember about their idea. We'll turn it into a personal link you can send them — so they can tell the full story themselves.
invite.f.ideaPlaceholder: An app that let farmers sell directly to Kabul markets
invite.f.name: Their name
invite.page.title: A friend thinks this idea deserves a place here.
invite.page.titleNamed: {name}, a friend of yours thinks this deserves a place here.
invite.page.body: Every project in this archive started with someone who kept going, even when it was hard. If this is yours — even if it stopped, paused, or never finished — it belongs here. Tell it in your own words.
invite.prefillNote: Prefilled from a friend's invite — check everything before you continue.
roadmap.title: Roadmap
roadmap.intro: Everything Qadamha can do today, and what we want to build next. If you'd like to help make any of the planned items real, see the Contribute page.
roadmap.shipped.title: Shipped
roadmap.shipped.1.title: Trilingual, right to left
roadmap.shipped.1.body: The whole site in English, Dari, and Pashto, with full right-to-left support.
roadmap.shipped.2.title: A searchable, filterable archive
roadmap.shipped.2.body: Every idea can be filtered by sector, city, year, status, and the reason it stopped.
roadmap.shipped.3.title: Search that understands Dari and Pashto
roadmap.shipped.3.body: Search matches spelling variants across scripts, not just exact text.
roadmap.shipped.4.title: Rich idea pages
roadmap.shipped.4.body: Founders and team credited with linked profiles, an archived-website preview card, related ideas, and related posts.
roadmap.shipped.5.title: People profiles
roadmap.shipped.5.body: A page for every founder and contributor, with their social links, credited on every idea they're linked to.
roadmap.shipped.6.title: Posts
roadmap.shipped.6.body: Stories, updates, opinions, and videos from the community — tied to an idea, or standing on their own.
roadmap.shipped.7.title: A year-by-year timeline
roadmap.shipped.7.body: Every idea placed against the history it happened in.
roadmap.shipped.8.title: Share and credit tools
roadmap.shipped.8.body: Share an idea's page to social apps, copy its link, or edit it directly on GitHub.
roadmap.shipped.9.title: Submit a story
roadmap.shipped.9.body: A no-account form that turns your words into a ready-to-send GitHub issue or email.
roadmap.shipped.10.title: Invite a friend
roadmap.shipped.10.body: Generate a personal link inviting someone whose idea belongs here to tell it themselves.
roadmap.shipped.11.title: Report an issue
roadmap.shipped.11.body: One click from the footer of every page.
roadmap.shipped.12.title: Share images for every page
roadmap.shipped.12.body: A preview image generated for every idea and post, in the right language and script.
roadmap.planned.title: Planned
roadmap.planned.1.title: Message the founder
roadmap.planned.1.body: Let visitors message an idea's founder directly from its page — including to ask them to pick it back up.
roadmap.planned.2.title: Show your support
roadmap.planned.2.body: Qadamha isn't only for ideas that failed. Many are only paused, or still quietly running through hard times. For any idea not marked as permanently stopped, add a button so visitors can show they'd back a restart — and for ideas still running, show they're behind it.
roadmap.planned.3.title: Donate
roadmap.planned.3.body: A donate button for Qadamha itself, and for the running or paused startups in the archive — plus a sponsors page on every idea showing who has backed it. Many ideas here stopped for lack of a few hundred dollars: modest money abroad, real money in Afghanistan.
